nodes.x: THERE was a man of the Pharisees, named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews:

Nicodemus, a Ruler of the Jews Certain insights from history shed further light on this man Nicodemus. (1) He was wealthy. He brought for the body of Jesus "a mixture of myrrh and aloes about an hundred pound weight." (John 19: 39). (2) Nicodemus was a Pharisee. This was an elite group of not more than 6000 brothers. To enter this brotherhood, men took a pledge in front of three witnesses that…

Is it true that the Brazen Serpent lifted up by Moses in the wilderness symbolized Christ? Why would the image of a serpent be used to represent the Savior? The use of the serpent as a symbol of divinity was used by many ancient cultures, including the descendents of the Book of Mormon people. In Mesoamerica, the feathered serpent, Quetzalcoatl (coatl meaning serpent), was for centuries used as a…

site of John's baptizing. Nephi recorded a prophecy from his father Lehi that John would "baptize in Bethabara, beyond Jordan." (1 Ne. 10:9.) Bethabara appears on the Medeba Map at the natural fording place east of Jericho entering Peraea. In Hebrew, Beth-abara or Beth-avara means place of crossing. friend of the bridegroom Jesus had come from God; he was the Son of God, Israel was his rightful bride and he was Israel's bridegroom.
